request.question: Test 10 nM FK506 effect on IL-2 secretion in Jurkat cells measured by ELISA

What is the numeric success threshold for IL-2 secretion?
What is the mechanistic rationale for using FK506?

hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Test 10 nM FK506 effect on IL-2 secretion in Jurkat cells measured by ELISA

references.title: Delta-opioid receptors expressed by Jurkat T cells enhance IL-2 secretion by increasing AP-1 complexes and activity of the NF-AT/AP-1-binding promoter element.
references.relevance_note: This paper discusses the enhancement of IL-2 secretion in Jurkat T cells, which is relevant to the measurable outcome of the hypothesis.

references.title: A COX-2 metabolite of the endogenous cannabinoid, 2-arachidonyl glycerol, mediates suppression of IL-2 secretion in activated Jurkat T cells.
references.relevance_note: This paper addresses IL-2 secretion in activated Jurkat T cells, providing context for the biological system and outcome of interest.

references.title: Somatostatin increases mitogen‐induced IL‐2 secretion and proliferation of human jurkat T cells via sst3 receptor isotype
references.relevance_note: This paper examines factors affecting IL-2 secretion in Jurkat T cells, which is pertinent to the hypothesis's focus on IL-2.

literature_qc.explanation: The intervention of FK506 is related to the biological system of Jurkat cells, as FK506 is known to affect T cell function, including IL-2 secretion. However, the specific concentration of 10 nM FK506 and the cell viability assay are not directly covered in the retrieved papers. Papers [3] and [4] discuss IL-2 secretion in Jurkat cells, indicating that similar experimental contexts exist, but they do not focus on FK506 specifically. Thus, while there is relevant work, it does not match the exact parameters of the hypothesis.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

risks.description: Control strategy may miss process-specific artifacts in handling or reagent preparation.

plan.objective: To determine the effect of 10 nM FK506 on IL-2 secretion in Jurkat cells.
plan.experimental_design: Jurkat cells will be treated with 10 nM FK506 for 24 hours. IL-2 secretion will be measured using an ELISA assay. Control groups will include untreated Jurkat cells and Jurkat cells treated with a vehicle control. The experiment will compare IL-2 levels between FK506-treated and control groups to assess the impact of FK506 on T cell function.
controls: Jurkat cells treated with 10 nM phorbol 12-myristate 13-acetate (PMA) and 1 µM ionomycin to stimulate IL-2 secretion.
Jurkat cells treated with vehicle (DMSO) without FK506 treatment.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Cell Preparation
Thaw Jurkat cells from liquid nitrogen storage and transfer 1 mL of the cell suspension into a 15 mL conical tube. Add 9 mL of RPMI 1640 medium supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin. Centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature. Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 10 mL of fresh RPMI 1640 medium.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge, 15 mL conical tubes, Pipettes and tips
Reagents: Jurkat cells (ATCC TIB-152), RPMI 1640 medium (Gibco 11875-093), Fetal Bovine Serum (FBS, Sigma F7524), Penicillin-Streptomycin (Gibco 15140-122)
Expected outcome: Cell viability >90% as assessed by trypan blue exclusion after resuspension.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 15 min | Cell Counting
Count Jurkat cells using a hemocytometer. Adjust the cell concentration to 1 × 10^6 cells/mL in RPMI 1640 medium.
Equipment: Hemocytometer, Pipettes and tips
Expected outcome: Cell concentration adjusted to 1 × 10^6 cells/mL.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Treatment Preparation
Dispense 1 mL of Jurkat cell suspension (1 × 10^6 cells/mL) into each well of a 12-well plate. Prepare FK506 treatment by diluting 10 µL of a 1 mM FK506 stock solution in 990 µL of DMSO to achieve a final concentration of 10 nM FK506. Add 1 mL of this FK506 solution to the appropriate wells. Include control wells with 1 mL of DMSO only.
Equipment: 12-well tissue culture plate, Pipettes and tips
Reagents: FK506 (Sigma F9292), DMSO (Sigma D8418)
Expected outcome: All wells receive the correct treatment concentrations.

DAY 1 | 24 h | Incubation
Incubate the 12-well plate at 37°C in a 5% CO2 incubator for 24 hours.
Equipment: 5% CO2 incubator
Expected outcome: Cells remain adherent and healthy after 24 h incubation.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Supernatant Collection
After 24 h, collect the supernatant from each well of the 12-well plate into separate 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes. Centrifuge at 1,000 × g for 5 min at 4°C to remove any cell debris.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge, 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes
Expected outcome: Clear supernatant collected for ELISA.

DAY 2 | 3 h | IL-2 ELISA
Perform IL-2 ELISA using the collected supernatants according to the manufacturer's protocol (e.g., eBioscience IL-2 ELISA kit). Prepare standards and samples in duplicate. Incubate the plate at room temperature for 2 h, followed by washing and adding detection antibody. Incubate for an additional 1 h, then add substrate solution and read absorbance at 450 nm using a microplate reader.
Equipment: Microplate reader (BioTek Synergy HTX), ELISA plate, Pipettes and tips
Reagents: eBioscience IL-2 ELISA kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Expected outcome: IL-2 concentration measured in pg/mL.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Analyze the IL-2 secretion data using GraphPad Prism software. Use a one-way ANOVA followed by Tukey's post-hoc test to compare IL-2 levels between FK506-treated and control groups. Report p-values and determine statistical significance.
Equipment: GraphPad Prism software
Expected outcome: Statistical significance determined with p < 0.05.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Jurkat cells are responsive to FK506 treatment at 10 nM concentration.
IL-2 secretion can be accurately measured using the chosen ELISA kit.
Cell viability is not adversely affected by FK506 treatment.
decision_criteria: Proceed if IL-2 secretion in FK506-treated cells is significantly higher than vehicle control (p < 0.05).
Proceed if cell viability remains above 80% after treatment with FK506.
Proceed if the ELISA standard curve shows R² > 0.95.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of FK506 for all experiments.
Maintain consistent cell passage number (less than 10 passages).
Calibrate the microplate reader before use.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: >=10 percentage points (pilot fallback) improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the effect of 10 nM FK506 on IL-2 secretion in Jurkat cells.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
